title: BNS Section 103 — Murder
body_md:
**Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023 — Section 103 (Punishment for Murder)**

(1) Whoever commits murder shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

(2) When a group of five or more persons acting in concert commits murder on the ground of race, caste or community, sex, place of birth, language, personal belief or any other similar ground, each member of such group shall be punished with death or imprisonment for life, and shall also be liable to fine.

*Mapping note:* Section 103 BNS replaces Section 302 IPC. Sentencing structure preserved; sub-clause (2) is new — codifies *Tehseen Poonawalla v. Union of India, (2018) 9 SCC 501* (lynching guidelines).

title: BNSS Section 482 — Bail (Anticipatory)
body_md:
**Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, 2023 — Section 482 (Direction for grant of bail to person apprehending arrest)**

When any person has reason to believe that he may be arrested on accusation of having committed a non-bailable offence, he may apply to the High Court or Court of Session for a direction under this section. That court may, if it thinks fit, direct that in the event of such arrest, he shall be released on bail.

Factors to consider include the nature and gravity of the accusation, the antecedents of the applicant, the possibility of flight from justice, and whether the accusation has been made with the object of injuring or humiliating the applicant.

*Mapping note:* Section 482 BNSS replaces Section 438 CrPC. Substantive principles unchanged; courts continue to follow *Sushila Aggarwal v. State (NCT of Delhi), (2020) 5 SCC 1*.

title: Section 138 NI Act — Cheque Dishonour
body_md:
**Negotiable Instruments Act, 1881 — Section 138 (Dishonour of cheque for insufficiency of funds, etc.)**

Where any cheque drawn by a person on an account maintained by him with a banker for payment of any amount of money to another person from out of that account for the discharge, in whole or in part, of any debt or other liability, is returned by the bank unpaid, either because of the amount of money standing to the credit of that account is insufficient to honour the cheque or that it exceeds the amount arranged to be paid from that account by an agreement made with that bank, such person shall be deemed to have committed an offence and shall, without prejudice to any other provision of this Act, be punished with imprisonment for a term which may be extended to two years, or with fine which may extend to twice the amount of the cheque, or with both:

Provided that nothing contained in this section shall apply unless—

(a) the cheque has been presented to the bank within a period of three months from the date on which it is drawn or within the period of its validity, whichever is earlier;

(b) the payee or the holder in due course of the cheque, as the case may be, makes a demand for the payment of the said amount of money by giving a notice in writing, to the drawer of the cheque, within thirty days of the receipt of information by him from the bank regarding the return of the cheque as unpaid; and

(c) the drawer of such cheque fails to make the payment of the said amount of money to the payee or the holder in due course of the cheque within fifteen days of the receipt of the said notice.

title: Section 9 Arbitration Act — Interim Measures
Mutual NDA — India SaaS template
body_md:
**MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT**

This Mutual Non-Disclosure Agreement ("**Agreement**") is entered into on [DATE] ("**Effective Date**") between:

(1) **[PARTY A]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, having its registered office at [ADDRESS] ("**Discloser/Recipient**"); and

(2) **[PARTY B]**, a company incorporated under [JURISDICTION], having its registered office at [ADDRESS] ("**Discloser/Recipient**").

**1. Purpose.** The Parties wish to explore a potential business relationship in connection with [PURPOSE] (the "**Purpose**"), and in connection therewith may disclose confidential information to one another.

**2. Confidential Information.** "Confidential Information" means any information disclosed by one Party ("Discloser") to the other ("Recipient"), whether orally, in writing, electronically or by inspection of tangible objects, that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and the circumstances of disclosure.

**3. Obligations.** Recipient shall: (a) hold all Confidential Information in strict confidence; (b) use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; (c) restrict disclosure to those of its directors, officers, employees, advisors and affiliates who have a need-to-know and who are bound by obligations of confidentiality at least as protective as those set out herein; and (d) not reverse-engineer, decompile or disassemble any software disclosed.

**4. Exclusions.** Confidential Information does not include information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available without breach by Recipient; (b) was rightfully in Recipient's possession prior to disclosure; (c) is rightfully obtained from a third party without breach of any confidentiality obligation; or (d) is independently developed by Recipient without use of Confidential Information.

**5. Term.** This Agreement is effective from the Effective Date and continues for two (2) years. Confidentiality obligations survive for five (5) years from the date of disclosure.

**6. Return / Destruction.** Within thirty (30) days of written request, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Information.

**7. Governing Law and Jurisdiction.** This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India. The courts at [CITY] shall have exclusive jurisdiction; provided that any dispute may, at the election of either Party, be referred to arbitration seated at Singapore under the SIAC Rules, with one (1) arbitrator and the language of arbitration being English.

**8. No Licence.** Nothing in this Agreement grants Recipient any right or licence under any intellectual property of Discloser, except the limited right to use Confidential Information for the Purpose.

**9. Remedies.** Recipient acknowledges that breach may cause irreparable harm to Discloser and that monetary damages may be inadequate. Discloser shall be entitled to seek equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in addition to any other remedies available.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

_________________________     _________________________
[PARTY A]                                    [PARTY B]
Name:                                              Name:
Title:                                                Title:

title: Companies Act 1967 (SG) — Section 157A (Director Powers)
body_md:
**Companies Act 1967 (Singapore) — Section 157A (Powers of directors)**

(1) The business of a company shall be managed by, or under the direction or supervision of, the directors.

(2) The directors may exercise all the powers of a company except any power that this Act or the constitution of the company requires the company to exercise in general meeting.

*Practice note:* The shareholder cannot, by ordinary resolution, override a properly-made board decision; this enshrines the *Automatic Self-Cleansing Filter Syndicate v. Cuninghame* principle into Singapore statute.

title: International Arbitration Act 1994 (SG) — Section 12A (Court-ordered interim measures)
body_md:
**International Arbitration Act 1994 (Singapore) — Section 12A**

(1) This section applies to a court in relation to arbitral proceedings whose seat is or will be in a State other than Singapore, as it applies in relation to arbitral proceedings whose seat is in Singapore.

(2) The High Court or a Judge thereof shall have the same power of making an order in respect of any of the matters set out in Section 12(1)(c) to (j) for the purpose of and in relation to an arbitration to which this section applies as it has for the purpose of and in relation to an arbitration to which this Part applies.

*Practice note:* Section 12A is the Singapore-court hook for SIAC-seated and even foreign-seated arbitrations — broadly equivalent to Section 9 of the Indian Arbitration Act, but available in support of foreign-seat proceedings as well.

title: Companies Ordinance (Cap. 622) — Section 465 (Director Duty of Care)
body_md:
**Companies Ordinance (Cap. 622) — Section 465 (Director's duty to exercise reasonable care, skill and diligence)**

(1) A director of a company must exercise reasonable care, skill and diligence.

(2) Reasonable care, skill and diligence means the care, skill and diligence that would be exercised by a reasonably diligent person with—

(a) the general knowledge, skill and experience that may reasonably be expected of a person carrying out the functions carried out by the director in relation to the company (the **objective** test); and

(b) the general knowledge, skill and experience that the director has (the **subjective** test).

*Practice note:* The dual objective+subjective test mirrors the UK Companies Act 2006 s.174 — a director with specialist expertise (e.g. a CFO with accounting qualifications) is held to the higher standard.

title: Civil Code (Japan) — Article 415 (Damages for Non-Performance)
body_md:
**Civil Code of Japan — Article 415 (Damages for non-performance of obligations)**

(1) If an obligor fails to perform consistent with the purpose of the obligation or if the performance of an obligation is impossible, the obligee may demand compensation for loss or damage arising from such failure; provided, however, that this does not apply if the failure to perform an obligation is due to grounds not attributable to the obligor in light of the contract or other source of the obligation and the common sense in the transaction.

(2) In the cases prescribed in the preceding paragraph, if any of the following items are met, the obligee may claim compensation for loss or damage in lieu of the performance of the obligation:

(i) the performance of the obligation is impossible;

(ii) the obligor manifests the intention to refuse to perform the obligation; or

(iii) the obligation has arisen from a contract, the contract is cancelled, or the right to cancel the contract has arisen due to the obligor's failure to perform the obligation.

*Translation note:* Reflects the 2020 amendment regime; pre-2020 translations may render this differently.

title: UAE Civil Transactions Law (Federal Law No. 5 of 1985) — Article 246 (Good Faith)
body_md:
**UAE Federal Law No. 5 of 1985 — Article 246 (Performance in accordance with the contract)**

(1) The contract must be performed in accordance with its contents and in a manner consistent with the requirements of good faith.

(2) The contract shall not be restricted to an obligation upon the contracting party to do that which is expressly contained in it, but shall also embrace that which is appurtenant to it by virtue of the law, custom and the nature of the disposition.

*Practice note:* Article 246 is the cornerstone of UAE contract performance — it codifies the civil-law good faith doctrine and informs all dispute resolution before UAE onshore courts. DIFC contracts are governed by DIFC Contract Law instead.

title: Cross-Border SaaS MSA — Pan-Asia template
body_md:
**MASTER SERVICES AGREEMENT (SaaS — pan-Asia)**

**1. Parties.** This Master Services Agreement ("**MSA**") is between [PROVIDER] ("**Provider**") and [CUSTOMER] ("**Customer**"), each a "Party" and together the "Parties", effective [DATE] ("**Effective Date**").

**2. Services.** Provider grants Customer a non-exclusive, non-transferable, worldwide right during the Term to access and use the SaaS services described in each executed Order Form ("**Services**").

**3. Subscription Fees.** Customer shall pay the fees set out in each Order Form. Fees are non-refundable except as expressly stated. All amounts are exclusive of GST/VAT/withholding taxes which shall be borne by Customer.

**4. Customer Data.** Customer retains all right, title and interest in Customer Data. Provider obtains a limited licence to host, process and transmit Customer Data solely to provide the Services. Provider shall implement administrative, physical and technical safeguards consistent with ISO/IEC 27001.

**5. Confidentiality.** Each Party shall protect the other's Confidential Information using at least the standard of care it uses for its own Confidential Information of like kind, but no less than reasonable care. Obligations survive three (3) years post-termination.

**6. IP.** As between the Parties, Provider owns all rights in the Services and any improvements thereto. Feedback is licensed to Provider on a perpetual, royalty-free, irrevocable basis.

**7. Warranties.** Each Party warrants that it has full power and authority to enter into this MSA. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY STATED, THE SERVICES ARE PROVIDED "AS IS" AND ALL OTHER WARRANTIES ARE DISCLAIMED.

**8. Limitation of Liability.** EXCEPT FOR (A) INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS, (B) CONFIDENTIALITY BREACHES, OR (C) GROSS NEGLIGENCE / WILFUL MISCONDUCT, EACH PARTY'S AGGREGATE LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED THE FEES PAID BY CUSTOMER IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM. NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES.

**9. Term & Termination.** Initial Term as stated in the Order Form. Either Party may terminate for material breach uncured for thirty (30) days after written notice. Customer may export Customer Data for sixty (60) days post-termination.

**10. Governing Law & Dispute Resolution.** This MSA shall be governed by [CHOICE OF LAW]. Any dispute shall be referred to arbitration seated in Singapore under the SIAC Rules, with three (3) arbitrators and the language being English. The seat of arbitration shall be Singapore.

**11. Notices.** All notices shall be in writing and sent to the addresses on the cover page.

_________________________     _________________________
[PROVIDER]                                  [CUSTOMER]
Name:                                              Name:
Title:                                                Title:

title: Section 9 Arbitration Application — Skeleton
body_md:
**IN THE HIGH COURT OF [STATE] AT [CITY]**
**O.M.P. (I) (COMM.) NO. ___ OF [YEAR]**

**IN THE MATTER OF:**

[APPLICANT] ... Applicant

VERSUS

[RESPONDENT] ... Respondent

**APPLICATION UNDER SECTION 9 OF THE ARBITRATION AND CONCILIATION ACT, 1996 SEEKING INTERIM MEASURES OF PROTECTION**

**Most Respectfully Showeth:**

1. The Applicant is a [DESCRIPTION OF PARTY], having its registered office at [ADDRESS]. The present application is being filed through [AUTHORISED REPRESENTATIVE], duly authorised vide Board Resolution dated [DATE], a copy whereof is annexed as **Annexure A-1**.

2. The Respondent is a [DESCRIPTION OF PARTY], having its registered office at [ADDRESS].

3. The Applicant and the Respondent entered into a [TYPE OF AGREEMENT] dated [DATE] ("the **Agreement**"). Clause [X] of the Agreement contains an arbitration clause (extracted at **Annexure A-2**) providing for resolution of disputes through arbitration seated at [SEAT].

**Brief Facts**

4. [Set out the facts giving rise to the dispute, in chronological order, in numbered paragraphs.]

**Cause of Action / Existence of Dispute**

5. [Plead the breach by Respondent and the Applicant's invocation of arbitration.]

**Grounds for Interim Relief**

6. The Applicant has a strong prima facie case on merits, as the Respondent has admittedly [BREACH PARTICULARS].

7. The balance of convenience lies entirely in favour of the Applicant inasmuch as [REASONS].

8. The Applicant shall suffer irreparable injury if interim protection is not granted because [REASONS — e.g. dissipation of assets, alienation of suit property].

9. Damages will not be an adequate remedy in the present case.

**Prayer**

It is, therefore, most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be pleased to:

(a) restrain the Respondent, by an order of injunction, from [SPECIFIC ACT];

(b) direct the Respondent to deposit a sum of Rs. [AMOUNT] in this Hon'ble Court / furnish a bank guarantee of equivalent value;

(c) appoint a court receiver to take charge of [PROPERTY];

(d) pass such other and further orders as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the present case.

**APPLICANT**

Through

[ADVOCATE'S NAME]
Counsel for the Applicant

Place: [CITY]
Date: [DATE]
